name: Grignols Historic Trail
name_original: Étang de Sigalens - boucle aux portes des coteaux Lot-et-Garonnais
description: This moderate 20.2-mile (32.6 km) touring cycling loop around Etang de Sigalens offers tranquil rides through hilly, wooded landscapes.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Aillas traverse the gentle, rolling countryside of the Gironde department in southwestern France. The area is characterized by its renowned vineyards, pine forests, and waterways, including the Bassanne river and the Canal latéral à la Garonne. Elevations range from 29 to 138 meters, offering varied terrain with gentle inclines and descents suitable for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Aillas The most popular touring cycling route is Vineyards of Marmandais Loop, a 29.2 miles (47.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through picturesque vineyard landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Grignols Historic Trail, a moderate 20.2 miles (32.6 km) path. This route explores historical sites and offers engaging terrain with gentle elevation changes. Local touring cyclists also love the Fontet Marina – Canal latéral à la Garonne loop from Loupiac-de-la-Réole, a 21.8 miles (35.1 km) trail leading through canal-side paths, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Touring cycling around Aillas is defined by vineyards, pine forests, and tranquil canal paths. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy family rides to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Aillas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Aillas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Aillas?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes around Aillas, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find 21 easy routes, 26 moderate routes, and 11 difficult routes to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain will I encounter on touring cycling routes in Aillas?
A: The terrain around Aillas is characterized by gentle, rolling countryside, with elevations ranging from 29 to 138 meters. You can expect varied landscapes including picturesque vineyards, tranquil pine forests, and serene canal paths along waterways like the Bassanne river and the Canal latéral à la Garonne. The routes offer engaging terrain with gentle inclines and descents, suitable for touring cyclists.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Aillas?
A: Yes, Aillas and the wider Gironde department offer many secure and accessible routes suitable for families. For an easier option, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41470328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Canal Latéral à la Garonne – Ship birth on the canal loop from Fontet&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 15.7 km ride along the canal.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see along the cycling routes?
A: Cycling around Aillas allows you to discover several interesting features. You can ride alongside the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2605415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne Lateral Canal&lt;/a&gt;, observe the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3968485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassanne Lock&lt;/a&gt;, and experience the charm of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3559952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Couthures-sur-Garonne Riverside Village&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also famous for its extensive vineyards and parts of the Landes de Gascogne Regional Natural Park with its pine forests.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Aillas?
A: Yes, many routes around Aillas are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1295263756/vineyards-of-marmandais-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vineyards of Marmandais Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 47.1 km ride through vineyard landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Aillas?
A: The Gironde department, where Aillas is located, is generally pleasant for cycling from spring through autumn. The mild climate allows for comfortable rides, especially when the vineyards are lush or during the harvest season. Avoiding the peak heat of summer afternoons might be advisable for longer rides.

Q: Are there historical sites or towns I can visit while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. Aillas itself is home to the 12th-century Église Saint-Martin d'Aillas-le-Vieux and Église Notre-Dame. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961517336/grignols-historic-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grignols Historic Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 32.6 km route specifically designed to explore historical sites. The wider Gironde department features medieval towns like La Réole and Bazas, which are often incorporated into cycling itineraries.

Q: Where can I rent a bike near Aillas?
A: While Aillas is a small commune, bike rentals are available in nearby towns such as Bazas, making it convenient to access equipment for your touring cycling adventures in the region.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Aillas?
A: The touring cycling routes in Aillas are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 190 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse scenery, from vineyards to canal paths, and the accessibility of routes for various skill levels.

Q: Are there routes that follow canals or waterways?
A: Yes, Aillas is close to the Canal latéral à la Garonne, and several routes incorporate these tranquil paths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35260356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontet Marina – Canal latéral à la Garonne loop from Loupiac-de-la-Réole&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 35.1 km trail that leads through canal-side scenery.

Q: Can I combine cycling with wine tasting in the Aillas area?
A: Definitely! Aillas is nestled within the renowned Bordeaux wine region. Many routes wind through picturesque vineyards, offering opportunities to visit châteaux for tours and tastings. The Entre-Deux-Mers region, with its rolling countryside, is particularly well-suited for combining cycling with wine exploration. You can find more information on vineyard cycling routes on the Bordeaux tourism website, such as this &lt;a href="https://www.bordeaux-tourism.co.uk/cycling-vineyards-routes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;overview of cycling in the vineyards&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there a longer, more comprehensive cycling itinerary that includes Aillas?
A: Aillas is part of the larger Gironde department, which is home to the "Grand Tour de Gironde." This comprehensive 480 km cycling itinerary showcases the region's diverse natural and cultural heritage, offering an extensive option for those looking for a multi-day touring experience. You can find more details about this tour on &lt;a href="https://en.francevelotourisme.com/cycle-route/the-tour-de-gironde-by-bike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;France Vélo Tourisme&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Small bucolic pond in the countryside, with picnic tables.
Former Saint-Martin de Monclaris church, built between the 12th and 14th centuries. Listed as historical monuments, in particular for its facades and roof.
Church of Saint-Martin de Gajac, from the 22nd century, listed as a Historic Monument.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Etang de Sigalens cycling loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, the loop typically starts and ends near the Lac de Sigalens. Look for available parking in the vicinity of the lake, which is a common starting point for activities in the area.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Etang de Sigalens cycling loop?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. It covers approximately 32.6 kilometers with an elevation gain of about 318 meters, requiring good physical condition. Be prepared for ascents and descents through hilly terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Etang de Sigalens cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly paved surfaces, but some segments may be unpaved. The area includes very small roads and dirt paths, which can become quite muddy after rain. You'll cycle through a hilly, oak-wooded landscape.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the tranquil Lac de Sigalens, a perfect spot for a break. You'll also pass by the Old church of Monclaris and the Saint-Martin de Gajac church. Keep an eye out for scenic viewpoints, such as from the old church at Campin, offering remarkable panoramas.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The average duration for this 32.6-kilometer touring bicycle route is approximately 2 hours and 5 minutes, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the potential for muddy sections on unpaved paths, it's best to cycle during drier seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, while avoiding the peak heat of summer and the wettest periods of winter.

faqs.question: Is the Etang de Sigalens cycling loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, specific regulations for dogs on this cycling loop are not provided. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially near roads or in areas with wildlife, and to be mindful of other cyclists and local residents.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned for cycling the Etang de Sigalens loop. It is generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or rest along the way?
faqs.answer: The Lac de Sigalens offers picnic tables, making it an ideal spot for a break. The route passes through a quiet, less-frequented region, so amenities like cafes or restaurants might be sparse directly on the loop. It's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks.

faqs.question: Is the starting point accessible by public transport?
faqs.answer: Information regarding direct public transport access to the starting point near Etang de Sigalens is limited. This area, bordering the Lot-et-Garonne hills, is generally more accessible by private vehicle. It's advisable to check local transport schedules if you plan to rely on public options.

faqs.question: What should I consider if cycling after rain?
faqs.answer: If cycling after rain, be aware that some unpaved segments and dirt paths along the route can become quite muddy and potentially more challenging. Ensure your bicycle is suitable for varied conditions and consider wearing appropriate gear for wet terrain.
